title: AgentRole
description: Activation-ready profile describing one agent role active in the repository. The profile records the role's identity, activation intent, scope, goals, allowed tools, scripts, cross-role invocation rules, restrictions, and cadence. Lives under agents/<role-id>.yaml. Deep authoritative protocols for each role live under docs/; the role profile references them via required_reading.

role_id.description: Lowercase kebab-case identifier. Must match the filename stem under agents/.

intent.description: Language-agnostic concept the agent matches against, regardless of exact user wording.

example_phrases.description: Informational only; not authoritative. Used by humans to recognise the role; the agent matches by intent.

purpose.description: Multi-paragraph explanation of why this role exists. The first thing a fresh agent reads after loading the file.

role_id.description: If the right role for this case is another defined role, name it here.

long_term.description: Durable goals that rarely change; describe the role's reason for existing.

current_targets.description: Optional configurable numeric / time-boxed targets for the current phase. May be adjusted without changing the role itself.

required_reading.description: Files the agent must load before acting in this role. Each entry must resolve to a real repository path; templated paths with <...> are forbidden.

typical_activities.description: Optional structured list of steady-state activities; each item is a title plus a short explanation.

allowed_tools.description: Generic capability descriptions. Must not embed absolute machine paths.

scripts_to_use.description: Repository script paths the role typically calls.

cadence.description: How often the role acts, and whether it reports back on a schedule or only on request.
